summary: Matched six particle functions in src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c, improved five additional particle report items, and corrected hsd_8039F05C/efLib_CreateGenerator linkNo typing to use s32/int rather than narrow integer types.

key_files.role: Primary matching work: six matched particle functions, ps_remove_node scheduling adjustment, ParticleConsoleState/ParticleScreenState local pointer use, generator ref_INC null-guard shape, hsd_8039F05C signature change, and remaining TODO update.

what_changed.text: hsd_8039F05C first parameter was widened from s8 to s32 in both implementation and header, and efLib_CreateGenerator widened its local linkNo from u8 to int.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: corrects first parameter from s8 to s32 and widens efLib_CreateGenerator local
raw_diff: particle.c hsd_8039F05C signature change
raw_diff: particle.h hsd_8039F05C prototype change
raw_diff: eflib.c linkNo u8 to int

what_changed.text: Several direct global aggregate accesses were rewritten through local state pointers, such as struct ParticleConsoleState* sp = &hsd_804CF7E8 and struct ParticleScreenState* sp = &hsd_804CF810.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_80393D2C introduces ParticleConsoleState* sp
raw_diff: hsd_803966A0 and fn_80397374 use ParticleScreenState* sp

what_changed.text: ps_remove_node changed the assignment/check sequence to if (*(head_ptr = &sp->xD0) == NULL), affecting inline scheduling for callers.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ps_remove_node hunk combines head_ptr assignment with null check
raw_diff: removed TODO comments citing ps_remove_node inline scheduling for hsd_80396C78 and fn_80397374

what_changed.text: hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC changed jobj assignment/ref_INC shape by adding an inner gen != NULL block and guarding ref_INC with jobj != NULL.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_8039EFAC hunk adds if (gen != NULL) and if (jobj != NULL) before ref_INC
raw_diff: hsd_8039F6CC hunk adds same guard shape

what_changed.text: hsd_80393D2C was wrapped in #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop.

smart_moves.text: The PR paired a prototype fix in particle.h with the implementation and caller local type changes, avoiding a mismatched call surface after widening linkNo.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: widened prototype removes spurious caller-side narrowing; efLib_CreateGenerator local widened so caller remains matched
raw_diff: eflib.c, particle.c, and particle.h linkNo changes

smart_moves.text: Using local state pointer variables for repeated global struct access reduced generated-looking repeated address expressions and contributed to reported matches.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: replacements of (&hsd_804CF810)->xBC and (&hsd_804CF810)->x1C with sp->xBC and sp->x1C
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: affected functions hsd_803966A0 and fn_80397374 reported 100.00%

smart_moves.text: The PR preserved a narrow unresolved note for hsd_8039F05C instead of claiming a match, updating the TODO from relocation differences to a .bss.0 section anchor/register-allocation issue.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a parameter convention is evident in surrounding APIs, correcting the callee prototype may require matching header and caller-local type updates to remove caller-side narrowing.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: hsd_8039F05C first parameter corrected from s8 to s32 matching int linkNo convention
raw_diff: particle.h and particle.c hsd_8039F05C signature changes
raw_diff: efLib_CreateGenerator linkNo changed from u8 to int

decomp_lessons.lesson: For repeated accesses to a global state struct, keeping a local typed pointer can be the matching shape and is more reviewable than repeated address-of global expressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_80393D2C introduces sp for hsd_804CF7E8
raw_diff: hsd_803966A0 and fn_80397374 introduce/use sp for hsd_804CF810
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: hsd_80393D2C, hsd_803966A0, fn_80397374 reported as new matches

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small source-shape changes inside an inline helper can resolve multiple callers that were previously blocked on inline scheduling.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ps_remove_node assignment/null-check reshaped
raw_diff: removed TODOs on hsd_80396C78 and fn_80397374 referencing ps_remove_node inline scheduling
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: hsd_80396C78 and fn_80397374 reported 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not treat byte-identical or partially improved unmatched functions as matched; keep a specific TODO that identifies the remaining suspected codegen or section-anchor issue.

raw_diff: struct ParticleScreenState* sp = &hsd_804CF810

assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: This is supported by the PR diff and the report showing hsd_80393D2C reached 100.00%, but the slice does not include adjacent-function objdiff details beyond the GALE01 report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use a local pointer to a global state struct to influence register/address reuse and avoid repeated direct global expressions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_80393D2C, hsd_803966A0, fn_80397374 local sp changes
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Consistent with typed-field and authored-source style standards; exact MWCC register reason is inferred from diff shape and match report, not explicitly explained in comments.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust inline helper scheduling by combining assignment and dereference in the null check.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: ps_remove_node if (*(head_ptr = &sp->xD0) == NULL)
raw_diff: removed TODOs citing addi/lwz order
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Evidence supports this as a matching tactic for ps_remove_node-related callers.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add redundant-looking gen != NULL block after an early null return, and jobj != NULL guard around ref_INC, matching dead-branch/null-check target shape in generator functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_8039EFAC guard shape
raw_diff: hsd_8039F6CC guard shape
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: both functions reached 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The prior TODOs referenced dead beq instructions, supporting the branch-shape motive.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep particle generator linkNo types consistent across callee, header, and callers
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2576, widening hsd_8039F05C linkNo from s8 to s32 was paired with the particle.h prototype update and efLib_CreateGenerator local widening from u8 to int; the PR body says this matches surrounding particle API convention and removes spurious caller-side narrowing.

raw_diff: hsd_8039F05C and efLib_CreateGenerator linkNo type changes

source_update_candidates.title: Particle generator APIs use int/s32 linkNo in this PR evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2576 states that hsd_8039F05C's first parameter was corrected from s8 to s32 to match the int linkNo convention used by surrounding particle APIs, and the diff updates particle.c, particle.h, and efLib_CreateGenerator accordingly.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md: int linkNo convention statement
raw_diff: linkNo type changes in particle.c, particle.h, eflib.c
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential subsystem path fact about linkNo typing; should be curated against current source and surrounding APIs before acceptance.

source_update_candidates.title: Scoped pragma exceptions need explicit match evidence
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2576 used a tightly scoped #pragma dont_inline block around hsd_80393D2C, and the GALE01 report showed that function as a new 100.00% match. This is consistent with the existing avoid-pragmas-register-asm and matching-tactics standards, but should not be broadened without more examples.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: scoped dont_inline pragma around hsd_80393D2C
text_corpus.jsonl issue_comment: hsd_80393D2C 100.00%
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible supporting example for existing standards, not a new standalone rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the redundant gen != NULL block after an early return as general style; evidence only supports it as a local branch-shape tactic for hsd_8039EFAC and hsd_8039F6CC.
Do not claim hsd_8039F05C matched; the report shows only an improvement to 80.79% and the diff retains a TODO.
Do not infer reviewer intent or requested changes; no review comments are loaded.
Do not treat #pragma dont_inline as broadly acceptable from this PR alone; the evidence is one scoped use with report support.
